title: CLAUDE.md
text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: NIL HMM Hidden Markov Model implementation calling introgressions Near Isogenic Lines (NILs) low-coverage sequencing data. project specifically designed maize genetics research, particularly BZea population (B73 × Teosinte crosses).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: main implementation organized Python package: introgression_hmm(): Main HMM implementation complex transition/emission matrices call_introgressions(): High-level interface coverage-specific parameter defaults read_vcf(): Converts VCF files genotype matrices write_results(): Outputs multiple file formats analysis Logging setup utility functions optimize_parameters(): Grid search HMM parameter tuning

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Handles argument parsing parameter configuration Provides coverage-level parameter presets (low/medium/high) Calls nilhmm.call_introgressions() user parameters scripts/parameter_tuning.py: Grid search parameter optimization scripts/preprocess_vcf.py: VCF preprocessing utilities

title: CLAUDE.md
text: docs/README.md: Detailed project documentation docs/jim_hmm_diagram.Rmd: R Markdown file HMM architecture diagrams docs/package_structure.md: Package organization design decisions

title: CLAUDE.md
text: envs/nilhmm.yml: Conda environment specification requirements.txt: Python package dependencies setup.py: Package installation configuration codemcp.toml: Development tooling configuration

title: CLAUDE.md
text: python_hmm_diagram.Rmd: Python HMM implementation details comparison R version File_S11_callIntrogressions.py: Original HMM implementation (basis nilhmm/core.py)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: HMM uses 3-state model sophisticated mathematical foundations:

title: CLAUDE.md
text: State 0: B73 homozygote (recurrent parent) State 1: Heterozygote (B73/Teosinte) State 2: Donor homozygote (Teosinte)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: 0: Major allele homozygote (minor allele count = 0) 1: Heterozygote (minor allele count = 1) 2: Minor allele homozygote (minor allele count = 2) 3: Missing genotype call

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Transition Matrix: Uses genetics-based probabilities incorporating recombination - recombination: P(S_t = | S_{t-1} = ) = 1-r - Recombination transitions weighted expected state frequencies Emission Matrix: Complex 3×4 matrix incorporating multiple error sources - Genotyping errors homozygotes vs heterozygotes - Non-informative marker effects - Missing data handling

title: CLAUDE.md
text: HMM uses 8 critical parameters must carefully tuned: nir: Non-informative marker rate (proportion markers don’t distinguish ancestry) germ: SNP calling error rate true homozygotes gert: SNP calling error rate true heterozygotes p: Proportion homozygous errors resulting heterozygous calls mr: Missing genotype call rate r: Recombination rate adjacent markers f_1: Expected frequency heterozygotes population f_2: Expected frequency donor homozygotes

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package provides optimized defaults different sequencing coverage levels: Low coverage (0.5-1x): nir=0.02, germ=0.08, gert=0.15, mr=0.20 Medium coverage (2-5x): nir=0.01, germ=0.05, gert=0.10, mr=0.10 High coverage (10x+): nir=0.005, germ=0.02, gert=0.05, mr=0.05

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Input: VCF files GT (genotype) field Processing: Convert numeric matrix (samples × markers) via nilhmm.io.read_vcf() Chromosome splitting: Process chromosome 1-10 separately HMM inference: Apply Viterbi algorithm per sample per chromosome via nilhmm.core.introgression_hmm() Output: Concatenate results across chromosomes save via nilhmm.io.write_results()

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Create conda environment conda env create -f envs/nilhmm.yml conda activate nilhmm  # Or install via pip pip install -r requirements.txt

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Install in development mode pip install -e .

title: CLAUDE.md
code: # Basic usage with automatic coverage detection python scripts/call_bzea_introgressions.py input_data.vcf  # Specify coverage level for parameter defaults python scripts/call_bzea_introgressions.py input_data.vcf --coverage low  # Custom parameters python scripts/call_bzea_introgressions.py input_data.vcf \     --nir 0.01 --germ 0.05 --gert 0.10 --p 0.5 --mr 0.15 --r 0.01  # Custom output prefix python scripts/call_bzea_introgressions.py input_data.vcf -o custom_results

code: # Format code codemcp format  # Run tests with coverage codemcp test-coverage  # Lint code codemcp lint  # Check git status codemcp status

title: CLAUDE.md
code: import nilhmm  # High-level interface results = nilhmm.call_introgressions(     vcf_file="data.vcf",     output_prefix="results",     coverage_level="low" )  # Lower-level access geno_matrix, marker_dict, samples, markers = nilhmm.read_vcf("data.vcf") calls = nilhmm.introgression_hmm(geno_matrix, marker_dict)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: VCF files must : - Standard VCF format (v4.0+) - GT (genotype) field FORMAT column - Chromosomes 1-10 (standard maize chromosomes) - Sample names header line - Markers sorted chromosome position

title: CLAUDE.md
text: pipeline generates four standardized output files: 1. *_introgression_calls.txt: Raw numeric matrix 2. *_introgression_calls.csv: Labeled calls headers 3. *_introgression_summary.csv: Per-sample summary statistics 4. *_marker_info.csv: Marker details (chr, pos, alleles)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Chromosome Processing: chromosome (1-10) must processed separately due linkage assumptions Marker Ordering: Input markers MUST sorted chromosome position HMM work correctly Missing Data Encoding: Uses integer 3 missing data, NaN Low Coverage Optimization: Parameters specifically tuned 0.5-1× coverage sequencing data

title: CLAUDE.md
text: extending codebase: - HMM relies hmmlearn.MultinomialHMM custom transition/emission matrices - Parameter values significantly impact results - use grid search optimization - genotype matrices use samples rows, markers columns - marker_dict maps chromosome numbers column indices genotype matrix - Core functions available package interface: nilhmm.call_introgressions(), nilhmm.introgression_hmm()

title: CLAUDE.md
text: project uses codemcp.toml configuration management, defines: - Code formatting linting standards (black, flake8, mypy) - Testing commands (pytest coverage) - Git automation settings - Python version requirements (>=3.8) - File ignore patterns configuration enables automatic commits (auto_commit = true) provides convenient development commands maintaining code quality.

what: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
title: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
text: nilHMM calls ancestry (donor introgressions) Near-Isogenic Lines sequencing data. single duration-aware 3-state (REF / HET / ALT) HMM engine two swappable axes: emission — count (BetaBinomial ref/alt read depths) gt (categorical genotype model explicit genotyping-error model), duration — geometric, rigidity (minimum-run-length prior), hsmm. choices, plus per-unit calling rule, express family named callers: nnil, rtiger, binhmm, atlas. package data-agnostic — every function takes (data, params) returns calls; pipeline scripts file paths sample lists. whole API one verb: nilHMM began Python package Jim Holland’s nNIL methodology; now R + Rcpp package. Python original frozen legacy/python/ (tag python-final).

title: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
text: Building source needs C++ toolchain (engine Rcpp / RcppParallel). rtiger caller Julia-free port RTIGER HMM, Julia install required. rebmix optional Suggests (bit-exact reproduction original binhmm clustering backend).

title: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
text: saturated-depth data trustworthy called genotypes (e.g. target sequencing), read VCF GT field instead let select categorical emission:
code: library(nilHMM)  # Read allelic counts (headerless "chr pos ref n_ref alt n_alt" TSV, or a # directory of them) into the engine's observation table, then call ancestry. calls <- call_ancestry(   read_counts("path/to/sample_counts.tsv"),   caller = "nnil",     # Holland's nNIL count caller   design = "BC2S2",    # breeding-design priors (f_1, f_2)   r   = 1e-4,          # recombination / geometric self-transition   err = 0.01           # baseline read error for the count emission )  # calls is the common segment schema: #   source, donor, name, chr, start_bp, end_bp, state   (state in {REF, HET, ALT}) head(calls) calls <- call_ancestry(read_vcf_gt("target.vcf.gz"),                        caller = "nnil", design = "BC2S2")   # g-only input -> gt emission

title: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
text: four share 3-state REF/HET/ALT chain design priors; differ emission, duration, input expect. nnil — count caller pools single-read observations along segment via BetaBinomial emission (err, conc); fit_means = TRUE EM-fits per-state alt fractions. Also runs categorical gt emission (Holland’s error model: germ, gert, p, mr, nir) called genotypes. rtiger — faithful reimplementation RTIGER rigidity HMM (EM + Viterbi, border re-placement), ported Julia; r integer rigidity (minimum run length). binhmm — bins genome (default 1 Mb), calls per-bin state anchored 3-state Gaussian-emission HMM (default cluster_method = "gauss" fixes HET -call high-coverage fragmentation), original GMM/k-means/rebmix clustering backends available reproduction. atlas — GOOGA-style caller transcript/competitive-alignment data: recurrent vs donor read fractions thresholded genotype calls, smoothed.

title: nilHMM — duration-aware HMM ancestry caller for NILs
text: read_counts(), read_vcf_gt() (/O); caller_spec(), emission_count(), emission_gt(), duration_geometric(), duration_rigidity(), duration_hsmm(), design_priors(), fit(), decode() (engine internals); calibrate_r(), select_emission() (calibration); plot_fragment_sizes(), write_common_schema() (output). See ?call_ancestry package documentation full reference.

title: Top-level ancestry-calling API — call_ancestry
text: Resolves named caller (optional design preset) emission + duration specs, runs fit() / decode() per (sample, chromosome), returns common-schema segment calls. Data-agnostic: pass observations ; function never reads paths.

title: Top-level ancestry-calling API — call_ancestry
code: call_ancestry(   data,   caller = c("nnil", "rtiger", "binhmm", "atlas"),   design = NULL,   r = 0.01,   err = 0.01,   conc = 20,   fit_means = FALSE,   p_switch = 0.01,   f_1 = NULL,   f_2 = NULL,   source = "nilHMM",   donor = NA_character_,   parallel = FALSE,   threads = 1L,   seed = 1L,   postprocess = TRUE,   emission = NULL,   bin_size = 1e+06,   cluster_method = c("gauss", "gmm", "kmeans", "rebmix"),   joint_clust = FALSE,   obs_weights = FALSE,   atlas_thresh = 0.95,   atlas_het = 0.25,   atlas_min_reads = 5L,   germ = 0.05,   gert = 0.1,   p = 0.5,   mr = 0.1,   nir = 0.01 )

title: Top-level ancestry-calling API — call_ancestry
text: data Long observation table columns name, chr, pos either n_ref, n_alt read counts (read_counts(); count/rtiger/binhmm/ atlas paths) pre-called hard-genotype column g {0,1,2,3} (read_vcf_gt(); auto-selects caller = "nnil", emission = "gt"). Optionally donor. caller One "nnil", "rtiger", "binhmm", "atlas". design Breeding-design key priors (e.g. "BC2S2", "BC2S3"). Required unless f_1/f_2 supplied. r, err, conc, fit_means, p_switch Caller parameters forwarded caller_spec(). Explicit formals (...) , e.g., r never partial-matched another argument. f_1, f_2 Single-locus priors, used design NULL. source Value output source column. donor Donor/taxon label data donor column. parallel TRUE, decode samples across cores via RcppParallel (fixed-means batched path; control thread count RcppParallel::setThreadOptions()). Identical results serial. threads, seed RTIGER caller : E-step threads seed randomized init. postprocess RTIGER caller : apply border re-placement (default TRUE). emission Optional emission override ("count", "gt") nnil caller; NULL uses caller's default. bin_size, cluster_method binhmm caller : genomic bin width bp (default 1 Mb) per-bin genotyping backend — "gauss" (default: anchored 3-state Gaussian-emission HMM; fixes HET -call high-coverage fragmentation K=3 clustering), original cluster--smooth route "gmm" / "kmeans" / "rebmix" (last needs suggested rebmix package, bit-exact rpubs reproduction). joint_clust/obs_weights apply cluster backends. joint_clust binhmm caller : TRUE, pool samples' bins learn one shared set REF/HET/ALT clusters raw alt-freq (borrows strength across cohort; cf. get_joint_ancestry_calls.R); FALSE (default), cluster sample independently informative-count-weighted alt-freq. joint clustering ; HMM stays per-sample. obs_weights binhmm caller , joint_clust = TRUE : TRUE, weight (gmm) clustering fit bin's informative-variant count (weights influence, alt-freq value). atlas_thresh, atlas_het, atlas_min_reads atlas caller : GOOGA genotype-call thresholds donor read fraction — homozygous call parent's fraction >= atlas_thresh (0.95), HET parents >= atlas_het (0.25), minimum atlas_min_reads (5) informative reads per gene (else missing). atlas, n_ref/n_alt recurrent/donor competitive-alignment read counts (ambiguous excluded upstream). germ, gert, p, mr, nir Genotype-error rates gt (categorical) emission (Holland's nNIL model): germ error true homozygotes, gert true heterozygotes, p fraction hom errors called het, mr missing rate, nir non-informative-marker rate. Raising germ/gert toward platform's real error rate native cure -fragmentation (isolated miscalled markers absorbed errors rather opening 1-marker segments); calibrate clean control, crank blindly. Used gt emission (emission = "gt", g genotype input, atlas caller).

title: Top-level ancestry-calling API — call_ancestry
text: data.frame common schema (source, donor, name, chr, start_bp, end_bp, state).

title: Top-level ancestry-calling API — call_ancestry
code: # Toy single-NIL count table: a REF stretch then a donor (ALT) block. set.seed(1) toy <- data.frame(   name  = "NIL1", chr = 1L, pos = seq_len(40L) * 1e5L,   n_ref = c(rpois(20, 8), rpois(20, 4)),   n_alt = c(rpois(20, 0), rpois(20, 4))) call_ancestry(toy, caller = "nnil", design = "BC2S2", r = 1e-4, err = 0.01) #>   source donor name chr start_bp  end_bp state #> 1 nilHMM  <NA> NIL1   1   100000 2000000     0 #> 2 nilHMM  <NA> NIL1   1  2100000 4000000     1

what: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
title: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
text: nnil    : Holland's nNIL – count/gt emission + geometric duration. rtiger  : rigidity mode – count emission + rigidity duration (S7). atlas   : GOOGA competitive-alignment caller – gt (categorical) emission geometric duration; per-unit call uses GOOGA fraction thresholds (applied call_ancestry()).

title: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
code: caller_spec(   caller = c("nnil", "rtiger", "atlas"),   r = 0.01,   err = 0.01,   conc = 20,   fit_means = FALSE,   p_switch = 0.01,   germ = 0.05,   gert = 0.1,   p = 0.5,   mr = 0.1,   nir = 0.01,   ... )

title: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
text: caller One "nnil", "rtiger", "atlas". r Duration hyperparameter: geometric self-transition rate nnil/atlas; integer rigidity (minimum run length) rtiger. err Count-emission baseline error. conc Count-emission BetaBinomial concentration. fit_means EM-fit emission means (count emission; S10). p_switch Free-state switch probability rtiger rigidity tail. germ, gert, p, mr, nir Genotype-error rates gt emission (atlas caller; Holland's nNIL error model): hom error, het error, hom-error->het fraction, missing rate, non-informative-marker rate. ... Ignored extra args (e.g. f_1/f_2 consumed call_ancestry()).

title: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
text: list(emission, duration).

title: Resolve a named caller into emission + duration specs — caller_spec
code: caller_spec("nnil", r = 1e-4)          # count emission + geometric duration #> $emission #> $type #> [1] "count" #>  #> $err #> [1] 0.01 #>  #> $conc #> [1] 20 #>  #> $fit_means #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "nilHMM_emission_count" "nilHMM_emission"       #>  #> $duration #> $type #> [1] "geometric" #>  #> $r #> [1] 1e-04 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "nilHMM_duration_geometric" "nilHMM_duration"           #>  caller_spec("rtiger", r = 5)           # count emission + rigidity duration #> $emission #> $type #> [1] "count" #>  #> $err #> [1] 0.01 #>  #> $conc #> [1] 20 #>  #> $fit_means #> [1] FALSE #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "nilHMM_emission_count" "nilHMM_emission"       #>  #> $duration #> $type #> [1] "rigidity" #>  #> $r #> [1] 5 #>  #> $p_switch #> [1] 0.01 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "nilHMM_duration_rigidity" "nilHMM_duration"          #>  str(caller_spec("atlas"))              # gt emission + geometric duration #> List of 2 #>  $ emission:List of 6 #>   ..$ type: chr "gt" #>   ..$ germ: num 0.05 #>   ..$ gert: num 0.1 #>   ..$ p   : num 0.5 #>   ..$ mr  : num 0.1 #>   ..$ nir : num 0.01 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "nilHMM_emission_gt" "nilHMM_emission" #>  $ duration:List of 2 #>   ..$ type: chr "geometric" #>   ..$ r   : num 0.01 #>   ..- attr(*, "class")= chr [1:2] "nilHMM_duration_geometric" "nilHMM_duration"

title: Decode the most-likely state path (Viterbi) — decode
text: Integer macro-state path markers (0 = REF, 1 = HET, 2 = ALT); rigidity expanded sub-states mapped back macro-states.

title: Decode the most-likely state path (Viterbi) — decode
code: obs <- data.frame(n = c(10, 9, 11, 8, 12), a = c(0, 0, 1, 4, 6)) model <- fit(obs, emission_count(), duration_geometric(1e-4),              priors = design_priors("BC2S2")) decode(model, obs) #> [1] 0 0 0 1 1

title: Rigidity duration (RTIGER reimplementation) — duration_rigidity
text: Enforces hard minimum run length r markers expanding state chain r sub-states (phase-type / Erlang machinery). Reimplements subset RTIGER use (BetaBinomial emission + rigidity + Viterbi + KS-calibrated r); optimize_R intentionally ported (-rigidifies, memory optimize-R-overrigidifies-nil-sim). Validate tests/fixtures/baseline_pre_refactor/rtiger_rigidity_ref/.

title: Rigidity duration (RTIGER reimplementation) — duration_rigidity
Rigidity duration (RTIGER reimplementation) — duration_rigidity
text: r Minimum run length markers (rigidity, integer >= 1). p_switch Per-marker switch probability free (post-minimum) state – geometric tail beyond enforced minimum run. Rigidity 1 reduces expansion plain geometric transition switch rate.

title: Rigidity duration (RTIGER reimplementation) — duration_rigidity
text: duration spec fit().

title: Rigidity duration (RTIGER reimplementation) — duration_rigidity
Count (BetaBinomial) emission — emission_count
text: Emission (n_ref, n_alt) read counts. State means theta; depth-0 markers emit flat. Means FITTABLE (EM-fit / reference-bias-corrected), fixed c(err, 0.5, 1 - err) – BRB run showed fixed theta_ALT = 1 - err collapses ALT->HET reference bias (S10, BRB_run_findings.md).

title: Count (BetaBinomial) emission — emission_count
Count (BetaBinomial) emission — emission_count
text: err Baseline genotyping/sequencing error (initialises theta). conc BetaBinomial concentration (overdispersion); near--op BRB (S10) retained regime axis. fit_means TRUE, EM-fit state means; FALSE (default) use fixed c(err, 0.5, 1 - err) reproduces Python baseline. Reference-biased data (RNA / BRB) needs TRUE (S10).

title: Count (BetaBinomial) emission — emission_count

title: Fit HMM emission/transition parameters — fit
text: fixed-mean emission (default, reproducing Python count caller) resolves theta transition otherwise -op. fittable-mean emission (fit_means = TRUE; required RNA / reference-biased data per S10) Baum-Welch EM-fits emission means. Viterbi/EM hot loops Rcpp.

title: Fit HMM emission/transition parameters — fit
text: obs per-(sample, chromosome) observation table emission's reader (count: columns n, ). emission Emission spec (emission_count() / emission_gt()). duration Duration spec (duration_geometric() / duration_rigidity() / duration_hsmm()). priors Single-locus genotype-frequency priors list(f_1, f_2). control EM control (max_iter, tol).

title: Fit HMM emission/transition parameters — fit
code: # Per-(sample, chromosome) count obs: n = depth, a = alt count. obs <- data.frame(n = c(10, 9, 11, 8, 12), a = c(0, 0, 1, 4, 6)) model <- fit(obs, emission_count(), duration_geometric(1e-4),              priors = design_priors("BC2S2"))

what: nilHMM: duration-aware HMM ancestry caller — nilHMM-package
title: nilHMM: duration-aware HMM ancestry caller — nilHMM-package
text: unified R + Rcpp engine calling REF/HET/ALT ancestry NILs sequencing data. One 3-state HMM swappable emission (emission_count(), emission_gt()) duration (duration_geometric(), duration_rigidity(), duration_hsmm()) layers expresses nnil rtiger callers (plus binhmm bin/cluster caller). See REFACTOR_R_PACKAGE.md design.

title: nilHMM: duration-aware HMM ancestry caller — nilHMM-package
text: package data-agnostic: hardcoded paths, sample lists, mount locations. Functions take (data, params) return calls; pipeline scripts (part package) files/samples outputs go.

title: nilHMM: duration-aware HMM ancestry caller — nilHMM-package
text: Maintainer: Fausto Rodriguez Zapata thegemmalab@ncsu.edu Authors: Fausto Rodriguez Zapata thegemmalab@ncsu.edu

what: Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
title: Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
text: Reads CollectAllelicCounts-style read counts (GATK-table readcount standard, memory gatk-table-readcount-standard) per-sample, per-marker (n_ref, n_alt, chr, pos) table count emission. TSV->counts adapter covers wideseq-thinned BRB inputs (cf. Python agent/brb_nilhmm_counts.py).

title: Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
text: path count TSV file, directory . format One "tsv" (chr pos ref n_ref alt n_alt headerless layout used skim/BRB counts), "gatk_table", "vcf_ad". name Optional sample name single file; defaults file's basename extensions stripped.

title: Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
text: long observation table: name, chr, pos, n_ref, n_alt.

title: Read allelic counts into the engine's observation table — read_counts
code: # Headerless "chr pos ref n_ref alt n_alt" TSV, as produced upstream. f <- tempfile(fileext = ".tsv") write.table(   data.frame(chr = "chr1", pos = c(1e5, 2e5, 3e5),              ref = "A", n_ref = c(8, 5, 0), alt = "T", n_alt = c(0, 3, 7)),   f, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE) read_counts(f, name = "NIL1") #>   name chr    pos n_ref n_alt #> 1 NIL1   1 100000     8     0 #> 2 NIL1   1 200000     5     3 #> 3 NIL1   1 300000     0     7

what: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
title: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
text: Extracts per-sample GT field biallelic diploid VCF (name, chr, pos, g) table categorical gt emission – Holland's nNIL genotype path, saturated-depth regime (e.g. MolBreeding target sequencing ~20x ) caller's called genotype trustworthy. g alt-allele dosage: 0 REF-hom, 1 het, 2 ALT-hom, 3 missing. Unlike read_counts() (feeds count/BetaBinomial emission AD read depths), reads called genotype directly, never touches AD.

title: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
text: path .vcf .vcf.gz file. GT must first FORMAT field (VCF spec requirement GT present). samples Optional character vector restrict subset samples.

title: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
text: long observation table: name, chr, pos, g.

title: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
text: Run call_ancestry(read_vcf_gt(path), caller = "nnil", emission = "gt", design = ...) (g-input auto-selects gt emission).

title: Read hard genotype calls (VCF GT) into the engine's observation table — read_vcf_gt
code: # A minimal biallelic-diploid VCF with a GT field. f <- tempfile(fileext = ".vcf") writeLines(c(   "##fileformat=VCFv4.2",   "#CHROM\tPOS\tID\tREF\tALT\tQUAL\tFILTER\tINFO\tFORMAT\tNIL1",   "1\t100000\t.\tA\tT\t.\t.\t.\tGT\t0/0",   "1\t200000\t.\tA\tT\t.\t.\t.\tGT\t0/1",   "1\t300000\t.\tA\tT\t.\t.\t.\tGT\t1/1"), f) read_vcf_gt(f)   # g in {0 REF-hom, 1 het, 2 ALT-hom, 3 missing} #>   name chr    pos g #> 1 NIL1   1 100000 0 #> 2 NIL1   1 200000 1 #> 3 NIL1   1 300000 2

what: RTIGER one-EM-iteration sufficient statistics (E-step + fold, all in C++) — rtiger_em_suffstats_cpp
title: RTIGER one-EM-iteration sufficient statistics (E-step + fold, all in C++) — rtiger_em_suffstats_cpp
text: Runs per-chain E-step (getlogpsi..gamma) accumulates pooled sufficient statistics, replacing slow R-level fold (apply()/tapply()). Mirrors fork's EM accumulation: transition band-sum zeta, start = Sum gamma column 1, per-state emission weights grouped distinct (k,n).

what: RTIGER full EM fit (E-step + M-step + convergence loop, all in C++) — rtiger_fit_cpp
title: RTIGER full EM fit (E-step + M-step + convergence loop, all in C++) — rtiger_fit_cpp
text: entire fit (port fork's fit/EM): per-chain rigidity E-step, pooled M-steps (transition, start, emission via C++ Brent), iterated max(|Δα|,|Δβ|) <= eps max.iter. Deterministic init (generate_params forms, randomize ). Returns fitted parameters iteration count.

title: RTIGER full EM fit (E-step + M-step + convergence loop, all in C++) — rtiger_fit_cpp
code: rtiger_fit_cpp(   ks_list,   ns_list,   r,   nstates,   eps,   max_iter,   threads,   init_alpha,   init_beta )

text: ks_list, ns_list Lists per-chain integer (k, n) vectors. r, nstates Rigidity number states. eps, max_iter Convergence tolerance iteration cap.

title: RTIGER forward pass (rigidity-aware Baum-Welch forward) — rtiger_forward_cpp
RTIGER forward pass (rigidity-aware Baum-Welch forward) — rtiger_forward_cpp
text: logPI length-s log start probabilities. logPSI s x (T+r) windowed-emission matrix (rtiger_productpsi_cpp). logA s x s log transition matrix. logpsi s x T log emission matrix (rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp). r Rigidity.

title: RTIGER forward pass (rigidity-aware Baum-Welch forward) — rtiger_forward_cpp
text: s x T log forward matrix (cols 1 >T-r+1 stay -Inf).

what: RTIGER gamma (state posteriors from zeta) — rtiger_gamma_cpp
title: RTIGER gamma (state posteriors from zeta) — rtiger_gamma_cpp
text: Literal port fork's gamma. Works linear space (zeta already exponentiated): first r columns normalized alpha*beta window edge; interior columns add windowed cumulative sum -diagonal zeta diagonal term; tail repeats; columns normalized sum 1.

title: RTIGER gamma (state posteriors from zeta) — rtiger_gamma_cpp
RTIGER gamma (state posteriors from zeta) — rtiger_gamma_cpp
text: zeta T x s x s array rtiger_zeta_cpp.

title: RTIGER gamma (state posteriors from zeta) — rtiger_gamma_cpp
text: s x T matrix state posteriors.

title: RTIGER emission log-probabilities (getlogpsi) — rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp
text: logpsi(,t) = logpdf(BetaBinomial(n_t, a_i, b_i), k_t). Memoized distinct (k,n) pairs (fork's getlogpsi cache; bit-identical values).

title: RTIGER emission log-probabilities (getlogpsi) — rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp
RTIGER emission log-probabilities (getlogpsi) — rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp
text: k Integer vector ref-allele counts (length T). n Integer vector totals (length T). , b Per-state BetaBinomial shape vectors (length s).

title: RTIGER emission log-probabilities (getlogpsi) — rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp
text: s x T matrix log emission probabilities.

title: RTIGER windowed emission product (productpsi) — rtiger_productpsi_cpp
text: PSI(,t) = sum psi window (t-r+1 .. t) (sliding window r), running cumulative sum. PSI s x (T+r): tail columns T+1..T+r-1 carry trailing partial sums column T+r left 0 (exactly fork).

title: RTIGER windowed emission product (productpsi) — rtiger_productpsi_cpp
RTIGER windowed emission product (productpsi) — rtiger_productpsi_cpp
text: logpsi s x T matrix rtiger_getlogpsi_cpp. r Rigidity.

title: RTIGER windowed emission product (productpsi) — rtiger_productpsi_cpp
text: s x (T+r) matrix.

what: RTIGER Viterbi (rigidity max-product + rigid backtrace) — rtiger_viterbi_cpp
title: RTIGER Viterbi (rigidity max-product + rigid backtrace) — rtiger_viterbi_cpp
text: Literal port fork's viterbi. step max : "stay" target state (diagonal), "enter" another state r positions back (using windowed PSI). backtrace fills whole r-block current state switch, giving r-rigid path. Tie-break first state (seed state 1, replace strict >), matching fork's findmax.

title: RTIGER Viterbi (rigidity max-product + rigid backtrace) — rtiger_viterbi_cpp
RTIGER Viterbi (rigidity max-product + rigid backtrace) — rtiger_viterbi_cpp
text: PI length-s log start probabilities. PSI s x (T+r) windowed-emission matrix. psi s x T log emission matrix. s x s log transition matrix. r Rigidity.

title: RTIGER Viterbi (rigidity max-product + rigid backtrace) — rtiger_viterbi_cpp
text: length-T integer state path (1-based states).

what: RTIGER zeta (pairwise posteriors over the rigidity window) — rtiger_zeta_cpp
title: RTIGER zeta (pairwise posteriors over the rigidity window) — rtiger_zeta_cpp
text: Literal port fork's zeta: build log values, normalize global max exponentiate (exp(-Inf - PO) = 0). Returned T x s x s array (column-major), non-log, Julia.

code: rtiger_zeta_cpp(logalpha, logbeta, logA, logPSI, logpsi, r)

text: T x s x s numeric array.

what: Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
title: Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
text: Selector rule (S5): saturated (>=~20x) -> gt; intermediate (~1-20x) -> count. Cost basis: BetaBinomial cost proportional number distinct (n, k) pairs, .e. coverage; ~20-30x count emission effectively hard call, gt equivalent cheaper (memory rtiger-betabinomial-cost).

title: Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
text: depth Mean per-marker sequencing depth.

title: Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
text: emission spec (emission_count() / emission_gt()).

title: Select an emission model from sequencing depth — select_emission
code: class(select_emission(2))    # ~2x  -> count emission #> [1] "nilHMM_emission_count" "nilHMM_emission"       class(select_emission(30))   # ~30x -> gt (hard-call) emission #> [1] "nilHMM_emission_gt" "nilHMM_emission"
